notes: Pickleheads lists exactly 3 Danville, CA venues: Osage Park (2 outdoor courts), Crow Canyon Country Club (8 outdoor courts), and Danville Station HOA (1 outdoor court). No single synthesized 'N locations / M courts' summary sentence for the Danville index page turned up via search, so pickleheadsStatedTotal above is a manual sum of those three individual listings (3 locations, 11 courts) rather than a directly-quoted Pickleheads aggregate — flagging that distinction for the human reviewer. All three are independently corroborated below by the Town of Danville's own site and/or other aggregators. A fourth real, currently-operating venue -- Diablo Vista Park, where the Town of Danville runs a pre-registration pickleball program on its 2 shared tennis courts -- was found via the Town's own official recreation pages (the primary source for this research) but has no Pickleheads page at all; it is included below with pickleheadsUrl: null. Danville has no existing entries in this repo's assets/courts-data.json or assets/venues.json and no cities/danville.html page yet, so every venue here is newly researched (matchStatus/existingId/conflicts fields omitted per this task's instructions rather than populated as 'new'/null/[]). Two apparent Danville pickleball venues surfaced repeatedly in general web searches for 'Danville pickleball' but are false positives from a same-named city in a different state, NOT Danville, CA: 'Danville Athletic Club' (2 indoor courts, $3 non-member fee) and 'Rosemary Helton Park' (multiple numbered pickleball courts, via danvilleparks.recdesk.com) are both confirmed to be in Danville, INDIANA 46122 (their own facility pages show IN addresses; one source is hosted on the danvillein.gov government domain). Neither is included here -- this is the exact 'state-collision gotcha' this repo's research methodology warns about. Likewise, a globalpickleball.network 'Danville, CA' page and a generic Yelp 'Danville, CA' pickleball search returned venues actually located in Walnut Creek, Pleasanton, Concord, Oakland, San Leandro, Martinez, Alameda, Hayward, and Brentwood (radius-based results, not actually in Danville) -- excluded. Swimply's 'Danville, CA' pickleball listings are private backyard courts individual hosts rent out by the hour, not public or membership-club venues comparable to the rest of this site -- excluded as out of scope. The most significant local storyline: Osage Station Park's pickleball courts were the subject of a real expansion fight. The Parks, Recreation and Arts Commission approved adding 6 new courts (2 -> 8 total) in December 2023, but a resident group ('Preserve Osage Park') formally appealed, citing noise, and the Town Council unanimously REJECTED the expansion on May 21, 2024, directing staff to explore sound mitigation (e.g. sound blankets) for the existing 2 courts instead. No source found indicates the project has been revived since; Osage Station Park is recorded here at its actual current 2-court count, not the never-built 8-court proposal -- do not let any source's 'up to 14' or '8' planning-stage figures overwrite this. Danville's own official Tennis Courts page (danville.ca.gov/223/Tennis-Courts) -- the primary source per this research's methodology -- lists exactly Diablo Vista Park and Osage Station Park as its public tennis/pickleball-adjacent facilities (plus two public high school courts with no pickleball connection found), corroborating that no other Town-run public pickleball venue was missed. Danville Station HOA and Crow Canyon Country Club are both private/membership facilities, included per this site's existing convention of listing membership clubs and HOA communities (e.g. Rossmoor in walnut-creek.json) as long as they're described accurately.

venues.name: Osage Station Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court -- dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets, converted from a former tennis court in April 2021
venues.hours: Courts open daylight hours, first-come-first-served. The Town of Danville's own Tennis Courts page lists this park's official court hours as Sun-Sat 8:00am-sunset (general park hours are 5:00am-10:00pm).
venues.weather: Inland San Ramon Valley climate -- hot, sunny summers (afternoon highs commonly 85-95F, sometimes topping 100F during heat waves) with cool mornings good for early play, mild and wetter winters, and far less coastal fog/wind than the Bay-adjacent cities to the west.
venues.notes: Pickleheads lists this venue as 'Osage Park'; the Town of Danville and every other source use the full official name 'Osage Station Park', used here. Court count (2, converted from 1 former tennis court, completed April 2021) and free/outdoor status are corroborated across Pickleheads, danvilletowntalks.org (citing Town meeting minutes), and tennispickleballcalifornia.com with no conflict, so confirmed:true. A 2022-2023 Town proposal to expand to 8 courts was approved by the Parks Commission in Dec 2023 but unanimously REJECTED by Town Council on May 21, 2024 after a resident appeal over noise -- see top-level notes; courts here reflect the current built reality (2), not that proposal. Minor unresolved conflicts not treated as blocking 'confirmed' (per this task's rule that only court count / indoor-outdoor are 'core facts'): Pickleballify's page for this same venue states a different address entirely (876 Orange Blossom Way, Danville) and claims lights + phone/online reservations, both of which contradict the Town's own tennis-courts page (no lights listed; first-come-first-served) and every other source checked -- treated as a Pickleballify data error and not used. lat/lon (37.8035, -121.9795) independently corroborated by two sources (a Google-sourced search snippet and mypacer.com) for the same 816 Brookside Dr address. No numeric Google Maps cid was found -- only a newer-format place_id (ChIJS2F94zrzj4ARj3Di1-Z54sk), which per this task's rule is not usable for googleMapsUrl, so left null.

venues.name: Diablo Vista Park
venues.surface: 2 shared outdoor hard-court tennis courts, taped/lined and fitted with portable nets for scheduled pickleball sessions -- not permanent dedicated pickleball courts
venues.netType: Portable nets set up for scheduled sessions on shared tennis courts
venues.price: Not stated for current sessions. A 2020 instance of this same Town-run pre-registration program charged $2 residents / $3 non-residents per session; not independently reconfirmed as current pricing for 2026.
venues.hours: Scheduled sessions only, pre-registration required (recent sessions offered 2:00-3:00pm and 3:00-4:00pm). General park/court hours per the Town's Tennis Courts page: Sun-Sat 8:00am-10:00pm.
venues.weather: Inland San Ramon Valley climate -- hot, sunny summers (afternoon highs commonly 85-95F, sometimes topping 100F during heat waves) with cool mornings good for early play, mild and wetter winters, and far less coastal fog/wind than the Bay-adjacent cities to the west.
venues.notes: Not on Pickleheads at all -- found via the Town of Danville's own official recreation pages (this research's primary source per the task methodology), not via Pickleheads or a secondary aggregator. This is a Town-run registered/scheduled program on the park's 2 existing shared tennis courts (taped lines + portable nets for the session), not permanent dedicated pickleball courts and not walk-up/anytime access -- described accordingly rather than as a standard walk-up venue. Confirmed still active as of the current 2026 Summer Activity Guide (specific session IDs #26543 / #26544 were cited by the Town's own guide at the same 2-3pm/3-4pm time slots used since at least 2020), so this is a genuinely currently-operating program, not a stale historical listing. Fee shown is sourced from a dated (October 2020) instance of this recurring program; no source gave a confirmed current 2026 fee, so it's presented with that caveat rather than as current pricing. Address ZIP is given as 94526 based on the majority of sources (Google Maps-sourced snippet, Yelp, a Town parking-directions PDF); one WebSearch summary paraphrase said 94506 but that could not be independently corroborated and looks like a transcription slip. googleMapsUrl's numeric cid was derived by converting the hex place ID (0xb2d29d3d5f7708b0) embedded in a Google Maps URL whose title explicitly matched 'Diablo Vista Park' -- not independently re-verified by loading maps.google.com directly, since that domain is not reliably fetchable by this research's tools.

venues.name: Bay Club Crow Canyon Country Club
venues.surface: Outdoor concrete -- dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.hours: Club hours: Mon-Fri 6:00am-9:00pm, Sat-Sun 7:00am-8:00pm (general club hours; pickleball-specific court hours not separately stated)
venues.weather: Inland San Ramon Valley climate -- hot, sunny summers (afternoon highs commonly 85-95F, sometimes topping 100F during heat waves) with cool mornings good for early play, mild and wetter winters, and far less coastal fog/wind than the Bay-adjacent cities to the west.
venues.notes: Named 'Bay Club Crow Canyon Country Club' here (matching bayclubs.com's own current name) rather than 'Crow Canyon Country Club', the pre-acquisition name Pickleheads still uses for its page (pickleheadsUrl reflects that older slug) -- The Bay Club's acquisition of this exact facility (same 711 Silver Lake Dr address) closed August 31, 2023, per Danville San Ramon Express reporting. The old-name Yelp listing ('Crow Canyon Country Club') is now marked CLOSED while the new-name Yelp listing ('Bay Club Crow Canyon') is active -- the facility itself did not close, only the branding changed, same pattern documented for ClubSport/Bay Club Pleasanton elsewhere in this repo's research. 8 outdoor concrete pickleball courts corroborated identically across Pickleheads, tennispickleballcalifornia.com, bounce.game, and maincourt.com under both names -- no conflict, so confirmed:true despite the name change. Membership required for any access; 'reservable: Walk-up' reflects Pickleheads' explicit statement that courts cannot be advance-reserved, i.e. members show up and play. lat/lon are third-party-geocoded (not a Pickleheads pin or direct Google Maps match) and two independent geocoders disagreed by roughly 500m for this large multi-amenity property, so approxLocation is set true even though the street address itself is firmly confirmed. No numeric Google Maps cid was found via search, so googleMapsUrl is left null per this task's rule.

venues.name: Danville Station HOA
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court -- pickleball lines overlaid on a tennis court
venues.netType: Tennis net height (not lowered to regulation pickleball height), used with overlaid pickleball lines, per Pickleheads
venues.price: HOA residency or a paid 'DSHOA Recreational Membership' required (specific fee not stated); non-member guests allowed only when accompanied by a member
venues.weather: Inland San Ramon Valley climate -- hot, sunny summers (afternoon highs commonly 85-95F, sometimes topping 100F during heat waves) with cool mornings good for early play, mild and wetter winters, and far less coastal fog/wind than the Bay-adjacent cities to the west.
venues.notes: Private, gated HOA residential community (318 homes, built 1973-1977), northwest of Crow Canyon/Greenbrook in Danville -- included per this site's existing convention of listing membership/HOA-restricted facilities (e.g. Rossmoor in walnut-creek.json) as long as access requirements are described accurately; this is NOT open-to-the-public. Court count is a genuine, unresolved conflict: Pickleheads states exactly 1 outdoor hard court with pickleball lines overlaid on a tennis court (taller net than regulation pickleball height); the HOA's own site (danvillestation.com/tennis-courts/) instead describes 'four lighted courts, perfect for a rally, team play, or a round of Pickleball' without specifying how many of the 4 are actually striped/usable for pickleball specifically. Left confirmed:false pending clarification of whether it's 1 court or up to 4. Address (1320 Van Patten Dr, Danville, CA 94526) is confirmed as the community's actual clubhouse/pool/tennis-court complex address (via Zillow, Trulia, and a Waze place listing for 'Danville Station' at that exact address) rather than merely the HOA management company's separate San Ramon mailing address (2266 Camino Ramon, San Ramon, CA -- that address belongs to the third-party HOA management office, not the physical courts, and is not used here). No reliable lat/lon geocode was found for the confirmed street address, so coordinates are left null with approxLocation:true rather than estimated. No numeric Google Maps cid found (only a newer-format place_id via Waze), so googleMapsUrl left null.
